data.description: Currently the layout is: Note -> 'Want to visit?' CTA + button. Should be: 'Want to visit?' CTA + button -> Note. Just swap the vertical order so the CTA is the primary attention-grabber and the note sits beneath it as supporting content. Scope: locate the section (likely on the campus or visit-related page) and reorder the two blocks in markup. Verify on both mobile and desktop that spacing/padding still reads correctly after the swap. Acceptance: 'Want to visit?' heading + button appear above the note block; visual hierarchy reads CTA-first; spacing intact at all breakpoints.
data.title: Flip order: 'Want to visit?' button should appear above the note, not below

data.body: Page confirmed: the 'Want to visit?' CTA + note that needs flipping is on the visit/neighborhood page (likely src/app/neighborhood/page.tsx or src/app/visit/page.tsx). NOT on the campus page — so this does not collide with FRAC-61 and can run in parallel.

data.reason: Bundled with FRAC-64 in same PR; impl committed both

provenance.reason: Bundled with FRAC-64 in same PR; impl committed both

data.body: Review pass. Verified against plan: in NeighborhoodPage.tsx the two adjacent <FadeIn delay={0.15}> siblings are swapped — the 'Want to visit? Fill out this form.' subtitle + Visitor Form Button block now precedes the MandelbrotCorners Note block inside the centered <div className='px-6 md:px-[4.5%] text-center'> container. No className changes on either block (Note retains border/rounded-md/bg/max-w-xl, CTA retains text-subtitle/Button max-w-xs w-full). Existing mb-3 md:mb-10 rhythm preserved. No other markup edits. pnpm typecheck clean. Visual ordering confirmation at /neighborhood deferred to human.

Cycle 2: reorder regression — after swapping Note and CTA, the original mb-3 md:mb-10 that sat between them moved below the Note. CTA now has no margin-bottom to push the Note away. Visitor Form button's Mandelbrot corners visually touch the Note container's top. Fix: add mb-3 md:mb-10 (or mb-6 md:mb-10 for more breathing room) to the CTA wrapper div.

data.reason: Cycle 2: CTA-to-Note spacing regression from the reorder

provenance.reason: Cycle 2: CTA-to-Note spacing regression from the reorder

data.body: Cycle 2 self-review: single-line className addition on the CTA wrapper div. Diff is one line. PR #214 ready for human visual QA on deploy preview.
